zh.lifeSummary: Lady Agnes Anne Masters是John Masters和Elizabeth Payne的女兒，於1510年出生在英國肯特桑德維其。她於1526年與Sir Nicholas Gifford在英國諾薩普頓史爾諾薩普頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1582年8月在英國諾薩普頓史爾享年72歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德登斯坦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Agnes Anne Masters是John Masters和Elizabeth Payne的女儿，于1510年出生在英国肯特桑德維其。她于1526年与Sir Nicholas Gifford在英国諾薩普頓史爾諾薩普頓结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1582年8月在英国諾薩普頓史爾享年72岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德登斯坦。

nameMeanings.name: Masters
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational and occupational name from Middle English (atte) maisteres ‘(at the) master's (house)’, a name for someone who lived or worked there, normally a servant. Compare Master and Masterman , and for the type compare Maidens , Monks , Parsons .
variant of Master , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Master
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Amar, Dhimant, Pravin, Amit, Aruna, Bhanu, Gaurang, Gopal, Haren, Jagu, Jayesh, Kalpesh.

English: occupational name from Middle English maister ‘master’ (Old French maistre). The word had a wide range of applications, and the reference in surnames may be to a schoolmaster, to the master of a farm or house, to the master of an apprentice, or to a ship's captain. In Scotland the eldest sons of barons and the uncles of lords were called Masters, but this is not primarily a Scottish surname.
German: dialect form of   Meister   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Maidens
meanings: occupational or habitational name from Middle English (atte) maydenes ‘(at the) maiden's or maidens' (house)’, a name for someone who lived or worked at such a house. Compare Masters and Parsons .
post-medieval variant of Maiden , with excrescent -s.

variants.name: Marsters
meanings: English: variant of Masters . Marsters is the predominant surname on Palmerston Island, Cook Islands, South Pacific, deriving from a settler and owner reputedly born in Leicestershire.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

variants.name: Anni
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Agnes Anne Masters was born in 1510, in Sandwich, Kent, England as the daughter of John Masters and Elizabeth Payne. She married Sir Nicholas Gifford in 1526, in Northampton, Northamptonshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. She died in August 1582, in Northamptonshire, England, at the age of 72, and was buried in Dunstan, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Agnes Anne Masters est née en 1510, à Sandwich, Kent, Angleterre, fille de John Masters et Elizabeth Payne. Elle a épousé Sir Nicholas Gifford en 1526, à Northampton, Northamptonshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en août 1582, à Northamptonshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterrée à Dunstan, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Agnes Anne Mastersは1510年にイギリスケントサンドウィチでJohn MastersとElizabeth Payneの娘として生まれた。1526年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアノーサンプトンでSir Nicholas Giffordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1582年8月にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアで72歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドダンスタンにて埋葬された。

ko.St James' Church, Thurning, Northamptons: 영국 노르섬프토실르 스른잉 스트 자메스' c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Agnes Anne Masters(은)는 1510년 영국 켄트 샌드위치에서 John Masters(와)과 Elizabeth Payne의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1526년 영국 노르섬프토실르 노르섬프턴에서 Sir Nicholas Gifford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1582년 8월 영국 노르섬프토실르에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 던스탠에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lady Agnes Anne Masters nasceu em 1510, em Sandwich, Kent, Inglaterra filha de John Masters e Elizabeth Payne. Ela casou-se com Sir Nicholas Gifford em 1526, em Northampton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em agosto de 1582, em Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, com 72 anos, e foi sepultada em Dunstan, Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.St James' Church, Thurning, Northamptons: ст джемес' чурч, турнинг, нортамптоншир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Lady Agnes Anne Masters родилась в 1510 г. в местности сэндвич, кэнт, Англия. Её родителей звали John Masters и Elizabeth Payne. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Nicholas Gifford в 1526 г. в местности нортамптон, нортамптоншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла август 1582 г. в местности нортамптоншир, Англия в возрасте 72 лет и была похоронена в местности дунстань, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Sir Nicholas Gifford en 1526, en Northampton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió en agosto de 1582, en Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 72 años, y fue sepultada en Dunstan, Northumberland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
